# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LOG_FILE="${LOG_FILE:-/tmp/aws-deploy-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log}"
DEPLOYMENT_TIMEOUT="${DEPLOYMENT_TIMEOUT:-600}"
AWS_REGION="${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1}"
ENVIRONMENT="${ENVIRONMENT:-development}"
DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-false}"

log() {
    local level="$1"
    shift
    local message="$*"
    local timestamp=$(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')
    echo "[${timestamp}] [${level}] ${message}" | tee -a "${LOG_FILE}"
}

error_exit() {
    log "ERROR" "$1"
    exit 1
}

check_dependencies() {
    log "INFO" "Checking dependencies..."
    
    local deps=("aws" "jq")
    for dep in "${deps[@]}"; do
        if ! command -v "${dep}" &> /dev/null; then
            error_exit "Required dependency '${dep}' not found. Please install it."
        fi
    done
    
    log "INFO" "All dependencies satisfied"
}

configure_aws_credentials() {
    log "INFO" "Configuring AWS credentials..."
    
    if [[ -n "${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID:-}" && -n "${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:-}" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "Using credentials from environment variables"
        export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="${AWS_REGION}"
        
    elif [[ -n "${AWS_PROFILE:-}" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "Using AWS profile: ${AWS_PROFILE}"
        export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="${AWS_REGION}"
        
    elif [[ -n "${AWS_ROLE_ARN:-}" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "Attempting to assume role: ${AWS_ROLE_ARN}"
        assume_role "${AWS_ROLE_ARN}"
        
    elif [[ -f "${HOME}/.aws/credentials" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "Using default AWS credentials from ~/.aws/credentials"
        export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="${AWS_REGION}"
        
    else
        error_exit "No AWS credentials found. Set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_PROFILE, AWS_ROLE_ARN, or configure ~/.aws/credentials"
    fi
}

assume_role() {
    local role_arn="$1"
    local session_name="${AWS_ROLE_SESSION_NAME:-aws-deploy-session-$(date +%s)}"
    local duration="${AWS_ROLE_DURATION:-3600}"
    
    log "INFO" "Assuming role: ${role_arn}"
    
    local credentials=$(aws sts assume-role \
        --role-arn "${role_arn}" \
        --role-session-name "${session_name}" \
        --duration-seconds "${duration}" \
        --output json) || error_exit "Failed to assume role"
    
    export AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID=$(echo "${credentials}" | jq -r '.Credentials.AccessKeyId')
    export AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=$(echo "${credentials}" | jq -r '.Credentials.SecretAccessKey')
    export AWS_SESSION_TOKEN=$(echo "${credentials}" | jq -r '.Credentials.SessionToken')
    export AWS_DEFAULT_REGION="${AWS_REGION}"
    
    log "INFO" "Successfully assumed role"
}

verify_aws_access() {
    log "INFO" "Verifying AWS access..."
    
    local caller_identity=$(aws sts get-caller-identity --output json 2>&1) || error_exit "Failed to verify AWS access: ${caller_identity}"
    
    local account_id=$(echo "${caller_identity}" | jq -r '.Account')
    local arn=$(echo "${caller_identity}" | jq -r '.Arn')
    
    log "INFO" "Authenticated as: ${arn}"
    log "INFO" "Account ID: ${account_id}"
    log "INFO" "Region: ${AWS_REGION}"
    
    echo "${account_id}"
}

validate_environment() {
    log "INFO" "Validating deployment environment: ${ENVIRONMENT}"
    
    local valid_environments=("development" "staging" "production")
    local is_valid=false
    
    for env in "${valid_environments[@]}"; do
        if [[ "${ENVIRONMENT}" == "${env}" ]]; then
            is_valid=true
            break
        fi
    done
    
    if [[ "${is_valid}" == "false" ]]; then
        error_exit "Invalid environment: ${ENVIRONMENT}. Must be one of: ${valid_environments[*]}"
    fi
    
    if [[ "${ENVIRONMENT}" == "production" ]]; then
        log "WARN" "Deploying to PRODUCTION environment"
        if [[ -t 0 && "${CI:-false}" != "true" ]]; then
            read -p "Are you sure you want to deploy to production? (yes/no): " -r
            if [[ ! "${REPLY}" =~ ^[Yy][Ee][Ss]$ ]]; then
                error_exit "Production deployment cancelled by user"
            fi
        fi
    fi
}

upload_to_s3() {
    local source="$1"
    local bucket="$2"
    local prefix="${3:-}"
    
    log "INFO" "Uploading to S3: s3://${bucket}/${prefix}"
    
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "[DRY-RUN] Would upload ${source} to s3://${bucket}/${prefix}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    aws s3 sync "${source}" "s3://${bucket}/${prefix}" \
        --delete \
        --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
        --output json 2>&1 | tee -a "${LOG_FILE}" || error_exit "S3 upload failed"
    
    log "INFO" "S3 upload completed successfully"
}

deploy_lambda() {
    local function_name="$1"
    local zip_file="$2"
    
    log "INFO" "Deploying Lambda function: ${function_name}"
    
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "[DRY-RUN] Would deploy ${zip_file} to Lambda function ${function_name}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    aws lambda update-function-code \
        --function-name "${function_name}" \
        --zip-file "fileb://${zip_file}" \
        --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
        --output json > /tmp/lambda-deploy.json || error_exit "Lambda deployment failed"
    
    local version=$(jq -r '.Version' /tmp/lambda-deploy.json)
    log "INFO" "Lambda function deployed successfully. Version: ${version}"
    
    log "INFO" "Waiting for function to be active..."
    aws lambda wait function-active \
        --function-name "${function_name}" \
        --region "${AWS_REGION}" || error_exit "Lambda function failed to become active"
    
    log "INFO" "Lambda function is active"
}

update_ecs_service() {
    local cluster="$1"
    local service="$2"
    local task_definition="$3"
    
    log "INFO" "Updating ECS service: ${service} in cluster: ${cluster}"
    
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "[DRY-RUN] Would update ECS service ${service} with task definition ${task_definition}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    aws ecs update-service \
        --cluster "${cluster}" \
        --service "${service}" \
        --task-definition "${task_definition}" \
        --force-new-deployment \
        --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
        --output json > /tmp/ecs-update.json || error_exit "ECS service update failed"
    
    log "INFO" "ECS service update initiated. Waiting for deployment to complete..."
    
    local end_time=$((SECONDS + DEPLOYMENT_TIMEOUT))
    while [[ ${SECONDS} -lt ${end_time} ]]; do
        local deployment_status=$(aws ecs describe-services \
            --cluster "${cluster}" \
            --services "${service}" \
            --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
            --output json | jq -r '.services[0].deployments | length')
        
        if [[ "${deployment_status}" == "1" ]]; then
            log "INFO" "ECS deployment completed successfully"
            return 0
        fi
        
        log "INFO" "Deployment in progress... (${deployment_status} deployments active)"
        sleep 15
    done
    
    error_exit "ECS deployment timed out after ${DEPLOYMENT_TIMEOUT} seconds"
}

invalidate_cloudfront() {
    local distribution_id="$1"
    local paths="${2:-/*}"
    
    log "INFO" "Creating CloudFront invalidation for distribution: ${distribution_id}"
    
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "[DRY-RUN] Would invalidate paths: ${paths}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    local caller_reference="deploy-$(date +%s)"
    local invalidation=$(aws cloudfront create-invalidation \
        --distribution-id "${distribution_id}" \
        --paths ${paths} \
        --output json) || error_exit "CloudFront invalidation failed"
    
    local invalidation_id=$(echo "${invalidation}" | jq -r '.Invalidation.Id')
    log "INFO" "CloudFront invalidation created: ${invalidation_id}"
    
    log "INFO" "Waiting for invalidation to complete..."
    aws cloudfront wait invalidation-completed \
        --distribution-id "${distribution_id}" \
        --id "${invalidation_id}" || log "WARN" "Invalidation wait timed out (this is normal for large invalidations)"
    
    log "INFO" "CloudFront invalidation completed"
}

deploy_elastic_beanstalk() {
    local app_name="$1"
    local env_name="$2"
    local version_label="$3"
    local s3_bucket="$4"
    local s3_key="$5"
    
    log "INFO" "Deploying to Elastic Beanstalk: ${env_name}"
    
    if [[ "${DRY_RUN}" == "true" ]]; then
        log "INFO" "[DRY-RUN] Would deploy version ${version_label} to ${env_name}"
        return 0
    fi
    
    aws elasticbeanstalk create-application-version \
        --application-name "${app_name}" \
        --version-label "${version_label}" \
        --source-bundle S3Bucket="${s3_bucket}",S3Key="${s3_key}" \
        --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
        --output json > /tmp/eb-version.json || error_exit "Failed to create application version"
    
    log "INFO" "Application version created: ${version_label}"
    
    aws elasticbeanstalk update-environment \
        --environment-name "${env_name}" \
        --version-label "${version_label}" \
        --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
        --output json > /tmp/eb-deploy.json || error_exit "Failed to update environment"
    
    log "INFO" "Environment update initiated. Waiting for deployment..."
    
    local end_time=$((SECONDS + DEPLOYMENT_TIMEOUT))
    while [[ ${SECONDS} -lt ${end_time} ]]; do
        local env_status=$(aws elasticbeanstalk describe-environments \
            --environment-names "${env_name}" \
            --region "${AWS_REGION}" \
            --output json | jq -r '.Environments[0].Status')
        
        if [[ "${env_status}" == "Ready" ]]; then
            log "INFO" "Elastic Beanstalk deployment completed successfully"
            return 0
        elif [[ "${env_status}" == "Terminated" ]] || [[ "${env_status}" == "Terminating" ]]; then
            error_exit "Environment is ${env_status}"
        fi
        
        log "INFO" "Deployment in progress... Status: ${env_status}"
        sleep 20
    done
    
    error_exit "Elastic Beanstalk deployment timed out"
}

cleanup() {
    log "INFO" "Cleaning up temporary files..."
    rm -f /tmp/lambda-deploy.json /tmp/ecs-update.json /tmp/eb-version.json /tmp/eb-deploy.json
}

main() {
    trap cleanup EXIT
    
    log "INFO" "Starting AWS deployment script"
    log "INFO" "Environment: ${ENVIRONMENT}"
    log "INFO" "Region: ${AWS_REGION}"
    log "INFO" "Dry run: ${DRY_RUN}"
    
    check_dependencies
    configure_aws_credentials
    local account_id=$(verify_aws_access)
    validate_environment
    
    case "${DEPLOYMENT_TYPE:-}" in
        s3)
            upload_to_s3 "${SOURCE_DIR}" "${S3_BUCKET}" "${S3_PREFIX:-}"
            if [[ -n "${CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID:-}" ]]; then
                invalidate_cloudfront "${CLOUDFRONT_DISTRIBUTION_ID}" "${CLOUDFRONT_PATHS:-/*}"
            fi
            ;;
        lambda)
            deploy_lambda "${LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME}" "${LAMBDA_ZIP_FILE}"
            ;;
        ecs)
            update_ecs_service "${ECS_CLUSTER}" "${ECS_SERVICE}" "${ECS_TASK_DEFINITION}"
            ;;
        elasticbeanstalk|eb)
            deploy_elastic_beanstalk "${EB_APP_NAME}" "${EB_ENV_NAME}" "${EB_VERSION_LABEL}" "${EB_S3_BUCKET}" "${EB_S3_KEY}"
            ;;
        *)
            log "WARN" "No DEPLOYMENT_TYPE specified or unsupported type"
            log "INFO" "Supported types: s3, lambda, ecs, elasticbeanstalk"
            log "INFO" "AWS authentication verified. Ready for deployment."
            ;;
    esac
    
    log "INFO" "Deployment completed successfully"
    log "INFO" "Log file: ${LOG_FILE}"
}

main "$@"
